hero.eyebrow: A warm color language for long-focus work
hero.title: Hearth Code
hero.subtitle: HearthCode is a low-glare color language for code interfaces. It works in Open VSX-compatible editors, VS Code, and Obsidian while keeping the same semantic hierarchy across dark and light variants.
hero.cta.hint: Pick your editor, install from the official channel, and start coding in minutes.
proof.label: What You Get
proof.title: One color language. Four tuned variants.
proof.subtitle: HearthCode keeps role mapping stable while tuning contrast and texture for different lighting environments.
proof.metric.1.label: Open VSX, VS Code Marketplace, and Obsidian
proof.metric.2.label: Dark, Dark Soft, Light, Light Soft
proof.metric.3.label: same token roles across all variants
proof.compare.caption: One fixed fixture rendered in all variants for clean release-to-release comparison.
proof.variant.dark.title: Hearth Dark
proof.variant.dark.body: Default daily driver with warm contrast and strong token hierarchy.
proof.variant.soft.title: Hearth Dark Soft
proof.variant.soft.body: Lower contrast pressure for late-night and dim-room sessions.
proof.variant.light.title: Hearth Light
proof.variant.light.body: Paper-toned light mode tuned for docs and daylight coding.
proof.variant.lightSoft.title: Hearth Light Soft
proof.variant.lightSoft.body: Softer light contrast for calmer daytime focus and long reading flow.
proof.cta.hint: Choose the editor you use today and install from its official channel.

philosophy.label: HearthCode Philosophy
philosophy.01.title: Ember, not flame
philosophy.01.body: High-saturation syntax colors burn bright but exhaust your eyes. Hearth keeps highlights in a moderate saturation range, then tunes them under readability constraints so they stay warm without glare.
philosophy.02.title: Neither black nor white
philosophy.02.body: Pure black backgrounds can create halation around white text and blur edges. Hearth uses warm, non-extreme background tones across all four variants to keep glare down and reading layers stable.
philosophy.02.swatchesAria: Background swatches for all theme variants
philosophy.03.title: Hierarchy as semantics
philosophy.03.body: Brightness maps directly to semantic weight: keywords are brightest, functions next, variables after, and comments deliberately fade into the background.
philosophy.04.title: Color temperature as comfort
philosophy.04.body: The entire palette is warm-toned, below 4500K. High blue-light content strains the retina. A warm coding environment is easier on your eyes over long sessions.
